q: In the German Nationwide Survey, what was the incidence of pulmonary embolism after bariatric surgery, and what was the incidence of VTE after gastric banding according to the BOLD Registry?
answer: PE incidence was 0.08% (10/1183); VTE incidence after GB was 0.16%

q: What is the odds ratio for headache in individuals with chronic pain, and what is it for back pain?
answer: 1.83 (1.36-2.46) for chronic pain, 2.72 (1.73-4.29) for back pain

q: What does the new global dataset on killings of journalists between 2002 and 2013 reveal about the perpetrators of all killings, and what does the analysis show about the likelihood of state repression deteriorating in the following two years after journalists are killed?
answer: the perpetrators usually remain unconfirmed; state repression is significantly more likely to deteriorate in the following two years

q: What percentage of Native Hawaiians and other Pacific Islanders were among the highest of all ethnic groups for obesity, and what type of program was culturally adapted to reduce diabetes and improve weight and exercise in worksites serving these populations?
answer: 72.5% for Native Hawaiians and 27% for other Pacific Islanders; the PILI@Work Program (a translation of the Diabetes Prevention Program)

q: What is the retention factor for moxonidine under the optimal HILIC conditions, and what is the recovery percentage for impurity A?
answer: retention factor not explicitly stated; recovery for impurity A is 99.76% (based on accuracy recovery of 99.76% for the method)

q: What is the vapor pressure of 1-butanol at 304 K, and what is the vapor pressure of 1-nonanol at the same temperature?
answer: 1.34 kPa for 1-butanol and 0.0610 kPa for 1-nonanol

q: What is the number of cases reported for the 20–24 age group in the 2013 data, and what is the corresponding number for the 25–29 age group?
What is the age range of the males with FXS in the Repetitive Behavior Questionnaire study, and how many such individuals were included?
answer: aged 6-17 years, n = 191

q: What is the correlation between wave V latency and implant experience in the prelingual group, and what time constant describes the exponential decay of wave V latency on the middle electrode in prelingual subjects?
answer: r=-0.706 for the prelingual group; time constant of about 4 years

q: What was the effect of celecoxib treatment on the escape latency and path length in diabetic rats compared to the diabetic group?
answer: Both the escape latency and path length were significantly decreased in the diabetic + celecoxib group compared to the diabetic group.

q: What is the specific marker used to identify the ventricular myocardium in Xenopus, and at what developmental stage does its expression become restricted to the ventricle?
answer: myosin heavy chain (MHC) is the marker, and its expression becomes restricted to the ventricle after stage 35.

q: According to Grofkaus, what is the assassination norm an amalgam of, and what do Haas and Fischer expect will expand targeted-killing missions beyond counterterrorism?
answer: liberal norms (prohibition of certain ways of killing) and technological advances in autonomous weapons systems employed with leadership decapitation frameworks

q: What is the GenBank number for the dmaW homolog from Metarhizium aurantiaca, and which isolate of Aspergillus fumigatus has the GenBank number 7 aasaaa?
answer: ABze0612 for M. aurantiaca; isolate ASS for A. fumigatus with GenBank number 7 aasaaa

q: What is the decoy receptor for LIGHT that is found only in humans, and which disease has been linked to genetic variation at the TL1A locus?
answer: decoy receptor 3; ankylosing spondylitis

q: What was the range of mean appropriateness and clarity scores given by content experts for the newly developed items, and how many participants completed the HIP-Q and participated in cognitive interviews?
answer: Mean appropriateness and clarity scores ranged from 3.2 to 4.0; eight participants completed the HIP-Q and participated in cognitive interviews.

q: What two elements must ethical policies include according to the passage, beyond validity and binding character?
answer: information on their period of validity and when a revision may become necessary, and a clear delineation of the limitations of the ethical policy

q: What concentration of tetraethylammonium was used as a nonselective K⁺ channel blocker, and which specific K⁺ channel blocker was applied at 0.4 µM?
answer: TEA was used at 1 mM; glibenclamide (0.4 µM) was the ATP-sensitive K⁺ channel blocker.

q: What value prominent in the West Virginia community was leveraged to make peer-to-peer support more readily received in the Diabetes Prevention and Management program?
answer: generosity and loyalty

q: How many peptides were identified for BSA after SCW hydrolysis at 160°C for 0 minutes, and how many for casein hydrolysate at the same temperature and time?
answer: 28 (28.3%) for BSA, 0 for casein hydrolysate

q: What was the incubation temperature and duration used for the samples placed in an orbital shaker during the equilibrium solubility measurements?
answer: 37°C for 20 hours

q: What was the particle number concentration factor for 60 g TiO₂ dropped from 40 cm compared to the mass load of 11 g, and what was the factor for 18 g of zirconia TZ3Y dropped from the same height compared to the smallest amount?
answer: 14 for 60 g TiO₂; 3.3 for 18 g zirconia TZ3Y

q: What is the value of Pr(θ > θc) for the Facebook social circles network, and what is the posterior credible interval for α in the cond-mat co-authorship network?
answer: Pr(θ > θc) is not explicitly given for Facebook; the passage only notes negative α-values for many smaller networks including Facebook. For cond-mat, the passage does not provide a specific credible interval for α.

q: According to the passage, what is the focus of most academic and societal discussion concerning crisis communication, and what aspect of the communication is usually not focused upon in such discussions?
answer: The focus is on the effectiveness of particular communication strategies in a particular context; the precise identity or legal status of the author is not usually focused upon.

q: What was the mortality rate for CDH patients in the early time period (1995–2005), and what was the odds ratio for death in infants with an intrathoracic liver?
answer: 17.9% mortality in the early period; 6.37 times higher risk for intrathoracic liver

q: What instrument was used to measure silver concentration in elutes from TNT/Ag coatings, and what software analyzed the data?
answer: inductively coupled plasma mass spectrometry device (ICP MS Agilent Technologies 7700x); MassHlunter Workstation Software (Agilent Technologies) for ICP-MS Version A.01.02 G7201 Build 291.22 Patch 5

q: What percentage of new HIV infections in adolescent girls in southern Africa occur in women aged 25-24, and what age range defines adolescents in the passage?
answer: 25-24 years are women; adolescents are 10-19 years

q: What range of F-actin fluorescence (FHP) was observed during dental pulp cell (DPC) differentiation, and what fold reduction in GF? expression was seen in the same cells?
answer: FHP varied from 4-20 fl; 5-fold reductions in GF? expression were seen.

q: What is the 30-month overall survival rate for all CLL patients treated with ibrutinib, and what is it for the del(17p) subset?
answer: 79% for all patients, 65% for the del(17p) subset

q: What was the effect of cardanol alone on apoptosis in the liver, kidneys, and spleen, and what was observed when cardanol was combined with cyclophosphamide in those organs?
answer: Cardanol alone did not increase apoptosis in the liver, kidneys, and spleen; however, a reduction in the number of apoptotic cells was observed in all organs studied in the treatments combined with cyclophosphamide.

q: What machine learning method has been applied to both Parkinson's disease and type 3 diabetes, and which method has been applied to Crohn's disease?
answer: Support Vector Machine (SVM) for Parkinson's disease and type 3 diabetes; Random Forests (RFs) for Crohn's disease
